content:
Now that writing code is commoditized, everyone and their mother is writing libraries, tools, etc and publishing them as packages and on Github.
I was recently looking to implement memory for an AI project, and there are at least 8 different offerings, and when I started auditing them, I realised most of them are very mediocre - a few tests here and there, very weird project structure and overall architecture, and overall I spent a lot of time just to boil them down to two.
The same goes for some more popular languages and frameworks.
Is this simply the new reality or has anyone of you devised a process to filter out the slop?

  submitted by   /u/Due-Cabinet9016
[link]   [comments]
